what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG icon showing a person outline with a plus sign to the right.
observations: The SVG uses viewBox 0 0 24 24 with explicit width and height of 24.
Rendering is stroke-based with fill="none" and stroke="currentColor".
Stroke styling uses width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
One path draws the person head and shoulder arc; a second path draws the plus sign.
standouts: The icon inherits color from currentColor.
The plus sign is drawn with simple vertical and horizontal line commands.
The person outline and add mark are separated into two path elements.
conventions: Uses a compact inline SVG structure.
Uses outline icon styling with rounded stroke terminals.
Does not include title, desc, role, or aria attributes in the SVG itself.
concerns: No embedded accessibility label or description is present.
notes: This file is a reusable outline person-add SVG icon that relies on the parent context for color and accessibility naming.
questions: Should accessibility text be supplied by the consuming component rather than embedded in the SVG?
Is the stroke width intended to remain fixed at 1.5 for this icon set?
purpose_vs_actual: The file name and path indicate a person-add icon, and the SVG content matches that by drawing a person outline plus an add symbol.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a scalable UI icon for adding or inviting a person while adapting to surrounding text or icon color.
contribution: Provides a compact, color-inheriting person-add icon substrate with a simple outline figure and adjacent plus mark suitable for reuse in fused interface systems.
summary_for_embedding: A 24px stroke-only SVG person-add icon using currentColor, rounded strokes, a person outline, and a plus sign.
intention_for_embedding: This asset was for representing an add-person or invite-person action as a reusable design-system icon.
